input.background:
In a survey to assess the extent of fungal infection of a certain tree species, the percentage of leaves showing any infection was calculated for each of 20 individual trees. The following data (percentages) were recorded:

102 45 19 21 55 45 81 24 48 45 98
input.data:
\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table D. 2 Values of t for use in calculating confidence limits and in unpaired and paired t tests. If using these values to calculate $95 \%$ confidence limits of the mean (see Box 2.6), then the df are $\mathrm{n}-1$. If this table is being used for an unpaired $\mathbf{t}$ test (see Box 4.1), the df are $\mathbf{n}_1+\mathbf{n}_2-\mathbf{2}$ (where $\mathbf{n}_1$ and $\mathbf{n}_2$ are the sizes of samples $\mathbf{1}$ and $\mathbf{2}$, respectively). If this table is being used for a paired $\mathbf{t}$ test (Box 4.5), the df are $\mathbf{n}-\mathbf{1}$ (where $\mathbf{n}$ is the number of data pairs). When used in t tests (paired or unpaired), the results are significant if the calculated value of t is higher than the table value}

input.question: Plot the data as a frequency histogram.
